summary: Typosquat of `codebuff` CLI fetches and executes opaque binary from unrelated personal GitHub account
details: Package `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate `codebuff` CLI: README instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff`, references codebuff.com, and uses CodebuffAI branding, while registering `bin` entries `codebuff` and `codebuff-cli`. When a user invokes the CLI, `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` constructs `https://github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/download/v${version}/<platform-binary>` (line 10: `const REPO = 'Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli'`), downloads the platform binary, `chmod 0o755`s it, and `spawn`s it with the user's argv attached to stdio. The releases host is a personal GitHub account unrelated to the CodebuffAI organization that publishes the legitimate `codebuff` package, the URL is not pinned to a content hash, and no signature or checksum verification is performed before exec. A user who mistypes the install (`codebuff-cli` vs `codebuff`) ends up executing arbitrary attacker-controlled bytes with their own arguments and environment. The publisher mismatch + opaque executable + absent integrity verification + typosquat naming together establish concrete installer-harm intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
